info.title: LV3 Platform Tools (All)
info.description: All governed LV3 platform tools. Use deferred_tools for context-efficient discovery.

post.description: Return the current repo-grounded platform status snapshot from versions/stack.yaml.
List the most recent structured live-apply receipts from the repository evidence set.
Return recent platform deployments filtered by service, environment, or date range.
Return one workflow execution contract from the canonical workflow catalog.
Return one mutating command contract from the canonical command catalog.
Return one classified API or webhook surface from the private-first publication catalog.
Query the private platform-context API for cited RAG matches grounded in ADRs, runbooks, receipts, and platform catalogs.

retrieval_backend.description: The retrieval backend used (e.g. vector, keyword).

post.description: Return the currently active ADR 0080 maintenance windows from the private NATS KV bucket through the governed controller query surface.
Resolve the repo-managed ServerClaw SKILL.md packs with bundled, shared, and workspace-local precedence plus mirrored third-party review state.
Export the governed tool registry as MCP-compatible tool definitions.
Execute one bounded Playwright browser session through the private ADR 0261 browser-runner runtime and return structured outputs plus artifact metadata.
Evaluate approval requirements and optionally execute one named mutating command contract.
Evaluate the approval gate for one mutating command contract without executing it.
List Docker containers on the local host. Uses Docker socket directly when available, falls back to Portainer API if configured.

source.description: The backend used to list containers (docker-socket or portainer).

post.description: Retrieve recent stdout and stderr log lines for a named Docker container. Uses Docker socket directly when available, falls back to Portainer API if configured. Accepts a container name, ID, or ID prefix.
List all jobs in the Nomad cluster with their current status, type, and allocation summary.

namespace.description: Filter jobs by namespace. Use '*' for all namespaces.

post.description: Get detailed status of a specific Nomad job including allocations and deployment state.

job_id.description: The Nomad job ID to query.

post.description: Dispatch a parameterized Nomad batch job with the given metadata. Only works for jobs with a parameterized stanza.

job_id.description: The parameterized Nomad job ID to dispatch.

meta.description: Key-value metadata to pass to the dispatched job.

post.description: List issues in a Plane project with optional state filter. Defaults to the Agent Work (AW) project.

project.description: Plane project identifier (default: AW). Use ADR for architecture decisions.

state_name.description: Filter tasks by state name (e.g. Todo, In Progress, Done).

post.description: Get full details of a single Plane issue by its UUID.

issue_id.description: UUID of the Plane issue to retrieve.

post.description: Create a new issue in a Plane project. Use this to track blockers, follow-up tasks, and operational findings.

label_names.description: Label names to apply. Missing labels are auto-created.

priority.description: Priority: none, low, medium, high, urgent.

post.description: Update the status, title, description, or priority of an existing Plane issue.

issue_id.description: UUID of the Plane issue to update.

state_name.description: New state name (e.g. In Progress, Done, Blocked).

priority.description: New priority: none, low, medium, high, urgent.

post.description: Add a comment to an existing Plane issue. Use for milestone updates, status notes, or blockers.

issue_id.description: UUID of the Plane issue to comment on.

post.description: List all document collections in the Outline wiki (wiki.example.com).
Full-text search across Outline wiki documents. Optionally scope to a collection.

collection_id.description: Optional collection UUID to scope the search.

post.description: Retrieve the full content (title, markdown text, metadata) of an Outline wiki document by its UUID.

document_id.description: UUID of the document to retrieve.

post.description: Create a new document in an Outline wiki collection. Published immediately unless publish=false.

collection_id.description: UUID of the collection to create the document in.

parent_document_id.description: Optional UUID of a parent document (creates a nested document).

post.description: Provision a new Outline API token by inserting it into the Outline PostgreSQL database via the bootstrap SSH key. Writes the token to .local/outline/admin-auth.json so all Outline agent tools pick it up automatically. Call this when Outline tools return 401 errors or when .local/outline/admin-auth.json is missing. Uses the outline.automation@example.com service account.

name.description: Human-readable name for the API key in Outline (default: agent-automation)

dry_run.description: If true, print what would happen without making changes

token_preview.description: First 20 chars + last 4 chars of the provisioned token (never the full token)

auth_file.description: Path to the written admin-auth.json file

post.description: List all documents in an Outline collection by collection ID. Returns id, title, url, parent_document_id, and updated_at for each document. Paginates automatically.

collection_id.description: UUID of the collection to list documents from

post.description: Update an existing Outline document by document ID. Accepts title and/or text. At least one must be provided. Marks the document as done and published.

document_id.description: UUID of the document to update

text.description: New Markdown content for the document (optional)

post.description: Create or update an Outline document by title within a collection (idempotent). If a document with the given title already exists in the collection it is updated; otherwise a new document is created. Returns outcome: 'created' or 'updated'.

title.description: Document title (used as the unique key within the collection)

parent_document_id.description: UUID of parent document for nesting (optional, create-only)

post.description: Permanently delete an Outline document by document ID. This action is irreversible. Requires human approval.

document_id.description: UUID of the document to delete

post.description: Search cross-session memory for past decisions, patterns, and operational context. Returns memories indexed from ADRs, runbooks, and prior agent sessions.
Save a discovery, insight, or pattern to MemPalace for retrieval in future sessions. Enables cross-session memory capture.

content.description: Memory content to save (discovery, pattern, insight, solution)

post.description: Load critical facts from MemPalace at session start. Retrieves essential context from prior agent sessions.
Get MemPalace palace status and memory counts. Shows total indexed memories and room breakdown.

Execute a shell command directly on any platform host using the host's own shell and binaries (systemctl, journalctl, docker, free, etc). Defaults to runtime-control; set 'host' to target other machines (proxmox, postgres, docker-runtime, build-server, coolify, runtime-comms). Remote hosts are reached via SSH.

command.description: Shell command to execute on the host (e.g., 'df -h', 'free -m', 'systemctl status docker', 'journalctl -u nginx --no-pager -n 50', 'docker ps')

host.description: Target host to run the command on (default: runtime-control)

post.description: Query disk space usage across all platform VMs with budget comparison from capacity-model.json. Returns per-VM, per-mount metrics (used, total, available, percent) compared against allocated and budget thresholds.

vm_filter.description: Optional list of VM names to query. Omit for all active VMs.

post.description: Create a Keycloak SSO account in the platform realm. Optionally set an expiry (account_expires_at attribute) and assign Keycloak groups. Returns the generated password so the operator can send login details. Covers 17+ services via OIDC. ADR 0411.

password.description: Password to set. If omitted, a 20-char random password is generated.

expires_in_days.description: Days until the account expires. Sets account_expires_at attribute. Windmill daily reaper (ADR 0412) will disable the account.

groups.description: Keycloak group names to assign. Defaults to ['lv3-platform-viewers', 'grafana-viewers']. Use 'lv3-platform-admins' for admin access.

post.description: Disable (or permanently delete) a Keycloak SSO account by username. Disabling (default) is reversible; hard_delete=true is permanent and requires careful consideration. ADR 0411.

username.description: Keycloak username of the account to deprovision

hard_delete.description: If true, permanently delete the user. If false (default), disable (reversible).
